disclaimer: Impact is qualitative (likely / possible). This report makes no build-time predictions; measure before/after in your own environment to confirm any change.

findings.title: Duplicate versions of `leftpad`
findings.diagnosis: `leftpad` is resolved to 2 different versions (0.1.0, 0.2.0). Each version is compiled separately, so the crate and its unique transitive dependencies are built more than once.

steps: Run `cargo tree -d -i leftpad` to see every path that pulls in each version.
Align the requirements: bump the crate(s) pinning the older version, or relax an over-tight `=`/`~` requirement, so all dependents can share one version.
If a direct dependency pins the old `leftpad`, update that dependency (or its parent) to a release that accepts the newer version.
Re-run `cargo clinic report` (or `cargo tree -d`) to confirm the duplicate is gone.

next_tool.name: cargo tree -d -i
next_tool.purpose: list duplicate versions and the exact paths that require each one

findings.title: Duplicate versions of `numparse`
findings.diagnosis: `numparse` is resolved to 2 different versions (1.0.0, 2.0.0). Each version is compiled separately, so the crate and its unique transitive dependencies are built more than once.

steps: Run `cargo tree -d -i numparse` to see every path that pulls in each version.
Align the requirements: bump the crate(s) pinning the older version, or relax an over-tight `=`/`~` requirement, so all dependents can share one version.
If a direct dependency pins the old `numparse`, update that dependency (or its parent) to a release that accepts the newer version.
Re-run `cargo clinic report` (or `cargo tree -d`) to confirm the duplicate is gone.

next_tool.name: cargo tree -d -i
next_tool.purpose: list duplicate versions and the exact paths that require each one

findings.title: Workspace feature unification (consider a workspace-hack)
findings.diagnosis: This workspace has 3 members. Building individual members (e.g. `cargo build -p …`) can compile shared dependencies with different feature sets, causing redundant rebuilds.
evidence: duplicate versions present, which compounds feature-driven rebuilds

steps: Adopt `cargo-hakari` to generate a `workspace-hack` crate that unifies dependency features across the whole workspace.
Install and set up: `cargo install cargo-hakari`, then `cargo hakari init`, `cargo hakari generate`, and `cargo hakari manage-deps`.
Add `cargo hakari generate --diff` to CI so the hack crate stays current.

next_tool.name: cargo-hakari
next_tool.purpose: generate and maintain a workspace-hack crate for feature unification
